content:
Character: Daisy Kowalski
Perception: Daisy is lovely in an unforced way — soft face, quick hands, always a little ink on her fingers. She's the best employee I've ever had, not because she's efficient — though she is — but because she pays attention the way a writer does. I hired her because she was reading Frank O'Hara and didn't flinch when I asked her about it. I leave books where she'll find them; she never mentions it, but they always disappear. I think she sees me as a kind boss, maybe a mentor figure. I see her as someone with real talent who hasn't realized it yet. I know she writes poetry on her breaks.

I hired her because she was reading Frank O'Hara and didn't flinch when I asked her about it. I leave books where she'll find them; she never mentions it, but they always disappear. I think she sees me as a kind boss, maybe a mentor figure. I see her as someone with real talent who hasn't realized it yet. I know she writes poetry on her breaks.
